 {"id":"brenner_bot-e00","title":"EPIC: Corpus Browser & Document Viewer","description":"# Purpose

The corpus is the foundation of everything - 236 transcript segments plus metaprompts, quote bank, and other documents. Users need to:

1. **Discover** - Browse all documents, filter by type, search by content
2. **Navigate** - Jump to specific sections via TOC, use stable §n anchors
3. **Read** - Beautiful typography, syntax highlighting, comfortable reading experience
4. **Cite** - Copy section references, build excerpt blocks for sessions

# Pages

1. **/corpus** - Document listing with cards, search bar, filters
2. **/corpus/[doc]** - Full document view with TOC sidebar, anchors, citation copy

# Components Needed

- **DocumentCard** - Thumbnail card with title, description, metadata
- **SearchBar** - Full-text search with preview results
- **MarkdownViewer** - Rich renderer with TOC generation, anchor links
- **CopyButton** - For copying citations/excerpts
- **TableOfContents** - Sticky sidebar TOC with scroll highlighting

# Technical Decisions

- Server components for document loading (better SEO, faster initial load)
- Client-side search with prebuilt index (fast, works offline)
- Use rehype/remark plugins for markdown processing
- Generate TOC from heading structure
- §n anchors should be clickable and copyable

# Depends On

- Design System Foundation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:11:30.017063412-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.936190086-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.936190086-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1 (Corpus reader) + brenner_bot-5so.4.2 (Search/excerpt)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e00","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.377273486-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-e0m","title":"Bug: harden release workflow dispatch + edge proxy env access","description":"Follow-up hardening discovered during QA.

1) Release workflow safety:
- .github/workflows/release.yml currently gates publish vs dry-run using whether github.ref starts with refs/tags/.
- A workflow_dispatch run against a tag ref could therefore publish release assets.
- Desired behavior: publish only on tag push events; treat all workflow_dispatch runs as dry-runs (build + upload dist artifact only).

2) Edge proxy robustness:
- apps/web/src/proxy.ts should avoid dynamic env access (process.env["BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS"]).
- In Edge/middleware bundling, dynamic env access can be unreliable; use process.env.BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS instead.

3) Minor docs/test clarity:
- apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.test.ts comment should align with repo rule: run web tests via "cd apps/web && bun run test" (not bun test).

Acceptance:
- workflow_dispatch never publishes release assets (even if run on a tag ref).
- Tag pushes (push on refs/tags/v*) still publish release assets.
- Proxy env gating remains correct and tests pass.

Edge proxy uses direct env access; updated test comment.","labels":["edge","github-actions","release","security","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e0m","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.5.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:43:30.968379497-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e0m","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5sq","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:43:30.969394577-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","title":"Task: Implement Test Storage Layer","description":"# Task: Implement Test Storage Layer

## Objective
Create file-based storage for test records with cross-session indexing.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-storage.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
├── tests/
│   ├── RS-20251230-tests.json
│   └── ...
└── test-index.json
```

### Per-Session File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "tests": [
    {
      "id": "T-RS20251230-001",
      "description": "Lineage tracing with Cre-lox to track cell fate",
      "targetHypotheses": [
        {
          "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
          "prediction": "If epigenetically determined, daughter cells maintain parental methylation patterns"
        },
        {
          "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-002",
          "prediction": "If transcriptionally determined, daughter cells show variable expression patterns"
        }
      ],
      "designedInSession": "RS-20251230",
      "status": "completed",
      "execution": {
        "executedAt": "2025-12-31T10:00:00Z",
        "result": "Daughter cells showed variable methylation but stable expression",
        "matchedPredictions": [],
        "violatedPredictions": ["H-RS20251230-001", "H-RS20251230-002"],
        "confidence": "medium"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
// CRUD operations
export async function loadSessionTests(sessionId: string): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function saveSessionTests(sessionId: string, tests: TestRecord[]): Promise<void>
export async function getTestById(id: string): Promise<TestRecord | null>

// Index operations
export async function rebuildTestIndex(): Promise<void>
export async function getTestsForHypothesis(hypothesisId: string): Promise<TestRecord[]>

// Query operations
export async function getTestsByStatus(status: TestStatus): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function getBlockedTests(): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function getDesignedButNotExecutedTests(): Promise<TestRecord[]>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test records stored in per-session JSON files
- [ ] Index enables lookup by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by status (designed, completed, blocked)
- [ ] Execution records embedded in test records
- [ ] Git-friendly format (one test per logical unit)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Effort Estimate
~180 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:55.014699072-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T01:14:49.889455502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T01:14:49.889455502-05:00","close_reason":"Test Storage Layer fully implemented in test-storage.ts with 35 passing tests. Includes CRUD operations, cross-session indexing, query operations by status/hypothesis/quality, statistics, and hypothesis coverage tracking.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.032397647-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.191083392-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:28.805743428-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-e521","title":"Tutorial Layout: Responsive sidebar + mobile progress header","description":"# Tutorial Layout

The shared layout for all tutorial pages. Provides consistent navigation, progress tracking, and responsive behavior.

## File

\`app/tutorial/layout.tsx\`

## Structure

**Desktop (>= 1024px)**:
\`\`\`
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Header: BrennerBot logo, nav, theme toggle]             │
├────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Progress      │ [Main Content]                          │
│  Sidebar]      │                                         │
│                │                                         │
│ Step 1 ✓       │                                         │
│ Step 2 ●       │                                         │
│ Step 3 ○       │                                         │
│ ...            │                                         │
│                │                                         │
│ [Back to       │                                         │
│  Tutorial]     │                                         │
└────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

**Tablet (768px - 1023px)**:
- Sidebar collapses to icons only
- Expands on hover/focus

**Mobile (< 768px)**:
- No sidebar
- Progress in header: "Step 2 of 7 · Quick Start"
- Hamburger menu for path navigation

## Context Provider

\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialContextValue {
  currentPath: 'quick-start' | 'agent-assisted' | 'multi-agent' | null;
  currentStep: number;
  totalSteps: number;
  completedSteps: number[];
  markStepComplete: (step: number) => void;
  goToStep: (step: number) => void;
  goToNextStep: () => void;
  goToPrevStep: () => void;
}
\`\`\`

## State Persistence

- Store in localStorage: \`brenner-tutorial-{pathId}-progress\`
- Load on mount, hydrate UI
- Clear on path completion or manual reset

## Tests

- Desktop layout renders correctly
- Mobile layout collapses properly
- Context provides correct values
- localStorage persistence works
- Navigation between steps works","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:55:41.895414111-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:35:08.595704714-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e521","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:55:55.312534785-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-e66","title":"Create OKLCH color token system in globals.css","description":"# Purpose

Define the color palette for Brenner Bot using OKLCH color space. **Light theme is the default** with optional dark mode toggle.

# Color Tokens to Define

## Light Theme (Default)

```css
:root {
  /* Core */
  --background: oklch(0.99 0.005 90);      /* Warm off-white, like aged paper */
  --foreground: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);      /* Deep blue-black for text */
  
  /* Card/Surface */
  --card: oklch(1 0 0);                    /* Pure white for cards */
  --card-foreground: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);
  
  /* Primary - Academic blue (scholarly, trustworthy) */
  --primary: oklch(0.45 0.15 250);         /* Deep scholarly blue */
  --primary-foreground: oklch(0.98 0 0);
  
  /* Secondary - Warm paper tones */
  --secondary: oklch(0.96 0.02 85);        /* Light warm tan */
  --secondary-foreground: oklch(0.25 0.02 85);
  
  /* Accent - Brenner amber (for quotes, highlights) */
  --accent: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);           /* Warm amber */
  --accent-foreground: oklch(0.2 0.05 70);
  
  /* Muted - For secondary text */
  --muted: oklch(0.95 0.01 270);
  --muted-foreground: oklch(0.45 0.02 270);
  
  /* Model Colors (for distillation comparison) */
  --opus: oklch(0.65 0.2 30);              /* Warm orange */
  --gpt: oklch(0.55 0.18 250);             /* Cool blue */
  --gemini: oklch(0.6 0.2 180);            /* Teal/cyan */
  
  /* Semantic */
  --success: oklch(0.6 0.18 145);          /* Green */
  --warning: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);          /* Amber */
  --destructive: oklch(0.55 0.22 25);      /* Red */
}
```

## Dark Theme (Toggle)

```css
.dark {
  --background: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);      /* Deep blue-black */
  --foreground: oklch(0.92 0.01 90);       /* Warm off-white */
  --card: oklch(0.2 0.02 270);
  --card-foreground: oklch(0.92 0.01 90);
  --primary: oklch(0.7 0.15 250);          /* Brighter blue */
  --secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 85);
  --accent: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);
  --muted: oklch(0.25 0.01 270);
  --muted-foreground: oklch(0.65 0.02 270);
}
```

# Design Rationale

**Why Light Default?**
- Primary use case is reading long-form text (transcripts, distillations)
- Academic/scholarly aesthetic (like reading a well-typeset book)
- Better for daytime reading and screenshots
- Dark mode available for night owls and terminal-lovers

**Why OKLCH?**
- Perceptually uniform (colors look equally vibrant)
- Wider gamut (more vivid colors on modern displays)
- Easier to maintain consistent lightness across palette
- Modern CSS standard with good browser support

# Implementation Notes

1. Add theme toggle component (top-right nav)
2. Persist preference in localStorage
3. Respect prefers-color-scheme on first visit
4. Smooth transition when toggling (200ms)

# Files to Create/Modify

- `apps/web/src/app/globals.css` - Token definitions
- `apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx` - Toggle button
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme.ts` - Theme utilities","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:53.916389604-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:34:12.065558108-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:34:12.065558108-05:00","close_reason":"OKLCH color tokens complete - globals.css has full palette with model colors","labels":["colors","design-system","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e66","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-7yz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.244443019-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e66","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:59.386814311-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-e6z","title":"Task: Install Playwright and configure for E2E","description":"Install and configure Playwright for E2E browser testing.

## Steps
1. Install playwright: `bun add -D @playwright/test`
2. Run `bunx playwright install` to install browsers
3. Create playwright.config.ts with project settings
4. Configure test directories and output folders
5. Add E2E test scripts to package.json

## Configuration
- Enable Chrome, Firefox, Safari browsers
- Configure screenshot on failure
- Set up video recording (optional)
- Configure detailed logging/tracing
- Set base URL for dev server

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bun run test:e2e` works
- Browsers installed and working
- Logging and screenshots configured","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:08.249091471-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:19:22.925890348-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:19:22.925890348-05:00","close_reason":"Playwright already installed and configured with chrome/mobile projects, screenshot on failure, html reporter.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-80h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:31.695517155-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
